summary:
Technology such as continuous glucose monitors does not by itself lead to improved glycaemic control; effective outcomes depend critically on ongoing patient education, lifestyle changes, and structured clinician involvement. Even with advanced devices enabling real-time data access, improvements in HbA1c are driven more by behavioral adherence and professional guidance than technological features themselves.
